machinery_genes.role: needle subunit (major filament protein of the injectisome needle)
machinery_genes.quote: Experimental evidence indicates that this protein, designated CdsF, has properties consistent with it being the major needle subunit protein. CdsF is concentrated in the outer membrane of elementary bodies and is surface exposed as a component of an extracellular needle-like projection
machinery_genes.note: Betts et al 2007, Chlamydia trachomatis. CdsF identified as the SctF/YscF-family needle subunit; the paper notes the SctF homologue had diverged beyond standard homology detection. CdsE and CdsG described in the same paper are needle chaperones (PscE/PscG-like) and are deliberately excluded as chaperones, not apparatus.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane ring, outer-ring component (basal body; YscD/PrgH family)
machinery_genes.quote: the T3SS inner membrane ring (IM-ring) of the basal body is formed by the periplasmic part of CdsD (outer ring) and CdsJ (inner ring)
machinery_genes.note: Merilainen et al 2016, Chlamydia trachomatis. CdsD = CT_664, C-terminal periplasmic part is YscD/HrpQ family; crystal structure homologous to Salmonella PrgH and Yersinia YscD. SctD-family inner-membrane ring.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane ring, inner-ring lipoprotein component (basal body)
machinery_genes.quote: the T3SS inner membrane ring (IM-ring) of the basal body is formed by the periplasmic part of CdsD (outer ring) and CdsJ (inner ring)
machinery_genes.note: Merilainen et al 2016, Chlamydia trachomatis (CdsJ = CTA_0609 in the companion crystallization paper PMID 25286957). SctJ/YscJ-family inner ring. Independently shown to be a core apparatus component by Fields et al 2003 (PMID 12694613): 'the core apparatus component CdsJ'.

machinery_genes.quote: EB-localized apparatus proteins such as CdsF, CdsD, and CdsC form higher-order complexes mediated by disulfide bonding
machinery_genes.note: Betts-Hampikian & Fields 2011, Chlamydia (C. trachomatis serovar L2 model). CdsC named as a structural T3SS apparatus protein; assigned SctC outer-membrane secretin role from the apparatus brief vocabulary and from Merilainen 2016 keywords listing CdsC alongside the IM-ring proteins. The CdsF/CdsD/CdsC quote also independently corroborates CdsF (needle) and CdsD (IM ring) as apparatus.

machinery_genes.quote: two hubs of protein-protein interactions that linked known secreted effector proteins to CdsQ, the putative cytoplasmic C-ring component of the secretion apparatus
machinery_genes.note: Spaeth et al 2009, Chlamydia trachomatis. CdsQ = SctQ/YscQ C-ring component. The Ct260/Mcsc and effector cargo it binds are chaperone/effectors, deliberately excluded; only CdsQ itself is listed as machinery.

machinery_genes.quote: Chlamydia pneumoniae possess a YscL ortholog, CdsL, that has been shown to interact with the T3S ATPase (CdsN)
machinery_genes.note: Stone et al 2011, Chlamydia pneumoniae (transfer within the same chlamydial Cds T3SS family; CdsN is conserved in C. trachomatis, gene CT_669). CdsN = SctN/YscN ATPase. Corroborated as the ATPase in Stone 2012 (PMID 22272312): 'the ATPase (CdsN)'.

machinery_genes.role: ATPase stator / regulator (YscL ortholog; negatively regulates CdsN ATPase)
machinery_genes.quote: Chlamydia pneumoniae possess a YscL ortholog, CdsL, that has been shown to interact with the T3S ATPase (CdsN)
machinery_genes.note: Stone et al 2011, Chlamydia pneumoniae (transfer within the chlamydial Cds family). CdsL = SctL/YscL stator that links to and down-regulates the CdsN ATPase. Functional, not a transcriptional regulator, so retained as apparatus.

machinery_genes.quote: CopD plays a critical role in pathogenesis and likely functions as a hydrophobic translocator of the type III secretion system in Chlamydia pneumoniae
machinery_genes.note: Bulir et al 2014, Chlamydia pneumoniae (transfer within the chlamydial Cds family; CopD conserved in C. trachomatis). Translocon proteins form the host-membrane pore and are apparatus per the brief, not effectors. Its putative chaperone LcrH_1 is excluded as a chaperone.

machinery_genes.quote: Chlamydia pneumoniae contains genes encoding two sets of translocator proteins; CopB and CopD, and CopB2 and CopD2
machinery_genes.note: Bulir et al 2014, Chlamydia pneumoniae (transfer within the chlamydial Cds family). CopB is the partner hydrophobic translocator of CopD. A second paralogous pair (CopB2/CopD2) exists; only the characterized CopB/CopD pair is listed. Translocators are apparatus per the brief.

notes: System: the Chlamydia injectisome (T3SS), an ancestral/divergent Sct-family system whose structural genes carry the chlamydia-specific 'Cds' (contact-dependent secretion) prefix and translocator genes the 'Cop' prefix. C. trachomatis D/UW-3/CX (NC_000117.1) carries the full cds gene set, but the molecular characterization literature is split: the basal-body / IM-ring / needle proteins (CdsD, CdsJ, CdsC, CdsF, CdsQ) are documented in C. trachomatis itself, whereas the ATPase complex (CdsN, CdsL) and the hydrophobic translocators (CopB, CopD) are best characterized in the closely related C. pneumoniae and transferred here within the same chlamydial Cds T3SS family (these genes are conserved across Chlamydia spp.). Status is PARTIAL rather than COMPLETE: several canonical Sct export-apparatus components were NOT given individual literature naming-quotes tying them to Chlamydia in this search and so are omitted to avoid hallucination, specifically the export gate SctR/S/T/U (Cds homologues), the major export ring SctV (CdsV/CT_060-region), the ATPase stalk SctO, the C-ring base SctK, the inner rod SctI, and the needle-length ruler SctP. Deliberately excluded as non-machinery: secreted effectors and Inc proteins (the 19 curated effectors for this instance), the needle chaperones CdsE and CdsG (Betts 2007), the C-ring-associated chaperone Ct260/Mcsc and its cargo (Spaeth 2009), the translocator chaperone LcrH_1 (Bulir 2014), and CopN, which although a SctW/YopN-like gatekeeper is primarily characterized in Chlamydia as a secreted regulator that plugs the channel and is itself injected, so it was conservatively excluded. Lowest-confidence call: CdsC's secretin (SctC) role is assigned from the apparatus brief and Merilainen 2016 keywords; its naming quote (Betts-Hampikian 2011) names it as a T3SS apparatus protein but does not itself spell out 'secretin'. CT/CTA locus tags appearing in notes (CT_664, CTA_0609, CT_669) are taken verbatim from the cited papers and are NOT used as the gene field; coordinate resolution is a separate scripted step. All quotes are verbatim from the cited PubMed abstracts/papers.
